page: Amazon Web Services m9g.8xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the custom AWS Graviton processor family. Built on the ARMv9 architecture, this instance features 32 dedicated physical cores utilizing the Neoverse-V3 model running at 2.8 GHz. It provides 128.0 GB of system memory, translating to a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB per core. The processor includes 64 KB of L1 data cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 48 MB of L3 cache, with hardware-accelerated AES cryptography support. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data highlights strong memory subsystem performance and efficient data processing capabilities. The instance achieves top-tier results in PassMark memory tests, static web serving throughput at 92.63 GB/sec, and single-threaded data compression tasks, with gzip compression reaching 36.59 GB/sec. Multi-core CPU performance is rated as strong, supported by a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 455.19 GB/sec and 92.33 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks. However, single-core compute and large-block uncached memory bandwidth remain in the average tier.

From a resource density perspective, the m9g.8xlarge offers a balanced vCPU-to-memory ratio typical of general-purpose instances, making it a cost-efficient option for workloads that do not require specialized GPU acceleration or high-speed local NVMe storage. The lack of simultaneous multithreading means each of the 32 vCPUs corresponds to a dedicated physical core, ensuring consistent execution times. A notable performance tradeoff is observed in LLM text generation, where the 135M model text generation speed falls into the weak tier, though prompt processing and medium-sized model text generation perform in the average to strong tiers.

The instance is suited for multi-threaded application servers, database operations, static web serving, and memory-intensive caching layers. Its high database operation benchmark scores and strong Redis throughput make it highly applicable for relational databases, key-value stores, and containerized microservices.
description: The Amazon Web Services m9g.8xlarge is an ARM64-based general-purpose instance powered by a 32-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V3 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core, and runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators but delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 17 Gbps. Benchmark results show top-tier performance in static web serving, database operations, and memory bandwidth, alongside strong multi-core processing and Redis throughput. Conversely, single-core performance and large-block uncached memory operations fall into the average tier, and small-model text generation is relatively weak. This instance is suited for multi-threaded application servers, database workloads, caching layers, and general-purpose containerized deployments requiring consistent physical core allocation.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m9g.8xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring 32 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V3 cores, 128 GB of RAM, and 17 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for databases and web serving.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m9g.8xlarge is a 32-core ARM64 general-purpose instance with 128 GB RAM, running on the Nitro hypervisor with no local storage.
tagline: A balanced 32-core ARM64 virtual machine delivering top-tier memory bandwidth and database performance for general-purpose enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated ARM64 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz on the AWS Nitro hypervisor
128.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio
Top-tier performance in database operations, memory bandwidth, and static web serving
17 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators
Suited for relational databases, caching layers, and multi-threaded application servers
